editor.slot_hint: Use this number with the connect IPC command, for example: connect 2.
editor.title: Device preferences

errors.bluetooth_audio_timeout: Bluetooth connected, but its audio output did not appear in PipeWire in time.
errors.bluetooth_connect_failed: Could not connect the Bluetooth device.
errors.bluetooth_device_not_found: The Bluetooth device was not found. Try scanning again.
errors.bluetooth_pair_failed: Could not pair the Bluetooth device.
errors.busy: Another audio operation is still running.
errors.command_start: Could not start the system command.
errors.device_not_found: The selected device no longer exists.
errors.device_unavailable: The selected device is not currently available.
errors.invalid_audio_data: The audio service returned invalid data.
errors.invalid_slot: The keybind number must be a whole number from 1 to 99.
errors.no_visible_devices: There are no visible devices to cycle through.
errors.pactl_missing: pactl is required but was not found on PATH.
errors.scan_in_progress: Bluetooth scanning is already in progress.
errors.slot_in_use: Keybind number {slot} is already assigned to another device.
errors.slot_not_found: No Bluetooth device is assigned to keybind number {slot}.
errors.switch_failed: Could not switch the default audio device.

panel.keybind_hint: Hidden devices are skipped by cycle-output and cycle-input. Bluetooth numbers can be used with the connect IPC command.
panel.no_visible_devices: All devices in this list are hidden.

scroll_step.description: How many percentage points each mouse-wheel step changes volume on the bar widget.
scroll_step.label: Scroll step

show_percentage.description: Show the current output volume percentage next to the bar icon. Disable it to show only the icon.
show_percentage.label: Show percentage

widget.tooltip:
Output: {output} ({output_volume})
Input: {input} ({input_volume})
Scroll: change output volume
Left click: open Audio Switcher
Right click: cycle outputs
Middle click: cycle inputs
